description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and four short, angled legs. The front face features two large, ornate dials on either side, likely for tuning and volume control, and a central frequency indicator with a clear scale marked from 5 to 16. Below the frequency display are several rectangular push buttons, probably for station presets or power. The top surface has a recessed, oval-shaped speaker grille with a mesh pattern, allowing sound to project upward. The wood finish is medium brown with visible grain, giving it a classic, mid-20th-century appearance.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with ornate dials, a central frequency display, and a mesh speaker grille on top.
description_view_0: Side view showing the rectangular wooden body and the edge of the top speaker grille.
description_view_1: Rear or back view displaying the plain wooden surface and the top speaker grille.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the wooden body and the edge of the speaker grille.
description_view_3: Front view highlighting the ornate dials, central frequency indicator, push buttons, and the speaker grille on top.
